candidate_problems.text: What concrete forms of criticism are sufficiently truth-conducive to distinguish explanatory success from stylistic plausibility in an LLM-centered system, and which proxy critics systematically fail this test?
Can a system built from token-predictive components search over conceptual structures rather than token strings in a way that measurably changes which explanations survive criticism?

Under what conditions does replacing human judgment with automated scoring cause optimization for seeming explanatory rather than being explanatory, and can this be detected from system behavior alone?
What memory and feedback mechanisms are required for recursive improvement to preserve genuine error-correcting gains rather than merely reinforce prior stylistic biases?
